content.problemStatement:
Chef wants to buy NNN (N≥2N \ge 2N≥2) flowers.

He can either pay 444 coins and receive 222 flowers, or pay 555 coins and receive 333 flowers.

Both types of transactions can be repeated as many times as he likes.

Find the **minimum** number of coins Chef needs to pay to buy **exactly** NNN flowers.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line, containing one integer NNN — the number of flowers Chef wishes to buy.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of coins Chef needs to pay to buy NNN flowers.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only option is to buy 222 flowers for 444 coins.
Test case 222: The only option is to buy 333 flowers for 555 coins.
Test case 333: Chef can buy 222 flowers for 444 coins and 333 flowers for 555 coins, for a total of 2+3=52+3 = 52+3=5 flowers at a cost of 4+5=94+5 = 94+5=9.
Test case 444: There are two options available to Chef:

Buy 222 flowers for 444 coins; four times.
This will result in 2+2+2+2=82+2+2+2 = 82+2+2+2=8 flowers at a cost of 4+4+4+4=164+4+4+4 = 164+4+4+4=16.
Buy 222 flowers for 444 coins once, and then buy 333 flowers for 555 coins two times.
This will result in 2+3+3=82+3+3 = 82+3+3=8 flowers at a cost of 4+5+5=144+5+5 = 144+5+5=14.

The second option is cheaper, so the answer is 141414.
